cases.question: What is the mission of the CIB Mango Tree project?
cases.ground_truth: CIB Mango Tree brings peer-reviewed research methods for detecting coordinated inauthentic behavior (CIB) on social media to journalists, fact-checkers, watchdogs, and researchers through an interactive analysis and dashboard app.

cases.question: Why is CIB Mango Tree built as a library of tests rather than a single test?
cases.ground_truth: No single test can definitively prove social media activity is coordinated or inauthentic, so the project provides many complementary tests that each highlight different aspects of activity.

cases.question: What GitHub organization and repository hosts CIB Mango Tree?
cases.ground_truth: The project lives at github.com/civictechdc/cib-mango-tree.

cases.question: What Python version is required to install CIB Mango Tree?
cases.ground_truth: Python 3.12 or newer is required (documentation specifies Python >= 3.12 and verifies 3.12.x).

cases.question: What software must be installed before setting up the CIB Mango Tree development environment?
cases.ground_truth: You need Python (>= 3.12), Git, and a command-line terminal.

cases.question: How do you clone the CIB Mango Tree repository?
cases.ground_truth: Run git clone https://github.com/civictechdc/cib-mango-tree.git and cd into the cib-mango-tree folder.

cases.question: How do you verify a successful CIB Mango Tree installation?
cases.ground_truth: Run python -m cibmangotree --noop; a successful install prints that the no-op flag was detected and exits successfully.

cases.question: How do you start the CIB Mango Tree application after setup?
cases.ground_truth: From the project root with the virtual environment activated, run python -m cibmangotree.

cases.question: What do pre-commit hooks do in the CIB Mango Tree repo?
cases.ground_truth: Pre-commit hooks automatically format code with Black and isort before each commit.

cases.question: What package manager does the installation guide recommend for faster dependency setup?
cases.ground_truth: uv is recommended as a fast Python package manager; use uv venv and uv sync --group dev to install dependencies.

cases.question: What operating systems and memory does CIB Mango Tree support for development?
cases.ground_truth: Supported OS includes Windows (PowerShell), macOS, and Linux; documentation recommends 4GB+ RAM and 1GB+ free storage.

cases.question: How is application data stored on macOS for CIB Mango Tree?
cases.ground_truth: On macOS the app uses ~/Library/Application Support/MangoTango/ for application data.

cases.question: Does CIB Mango Tree require manual database setup on first run?
cases.ground_truth: No manual database setup is required; TinyDB is initialized automatically on first run.

cases.question: How can a new contributor join the CIB Mango Tree community online?
cases.ground_truth: Join Civic Tech DC Slack (civictechdc.org/slack) and look for cib-mango-tree channels; virtual participation is also welcome.

cases.question: What is the hand-off deliverable when researching a new CIB Mango Tree test?
cases.ground_truth: Fill out the test outline template on Google Drive to propose a new test, recording references and a starting point for implementation.

cases.question: What is the hand-off when implementing a new analyzer in CIB Mango Tree?
cases.ground_truth: Open a pull request to the main civictechdc/cib-mango-tree repository that adds the new analyzer code.

cases.question: What should you deliver after prototyping a new CIB Mango Tree test?
cases.ground_truth: Open a GitHub issue on the main repository describing the prototyped test as a feature request, noting what worked and what did not.

cases.question: Where should Cursor users look for AI-assisted development instructions in CIB Mango Tree?
cases.ground_truth: Cursor users should read .cursorrules and the .ai-context/ directory; documentation notes AI rules may be outdated and under review.

cases.question: What Python version fixes common pyarrow dependency install failures?
cases.ground_truth: Use Python 3.12; pyarrow install failures are often due to version mismatches with other Python versions.

cases.question: What is the capital of Atlantis in the year 3026?
cases.ground_truth: The corpus does not contain information about Atlantis; the system should indicate insufficient or no matching context.
